original_hypothesis.title: transferFrom Fee Deduction Bypasses ERC20 Allowance, Draining Sender Beyond Approval
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: _payTxFee deducts transaction fees directly from the sender's balance via _update() without consuming any allowance. Then super.transferFrom() only checks and consumes allowance for the transfer amount. Total tokens deducted from sender = amount + fees, but allowance only covers amount. Any approved spender can force the sender to lose more tokens than approved.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This violates the core ERC20 allowance invariant. At 100% fee rate (allowed by setTxFeeRate), an approval of N tokens lets the spender extract 2N tokens from the sender. Even at normal fee rates, every transferFrom extracts extra tokens beyond what the sender authorized.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _payTxFee calls _update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) which bypasses allowance; then super.transferFrom only deducts 'amount' from allowance

preconditions: Sender has approved spender for some amount
Sender has sufficient balance for amount + fees

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: _update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) deducts fees without allowance check; super.transferFrom only consumes 'amount' from allowance
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Total tokens removed from sender (amount + txFees) exceeds the allowance approved by sender
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No mechanism to include fees in allowance consumption or check total deduction against allowance

analysis_questions: Can a spender repeatedly call transferFrom to drain sender beyond initial approval?
What is the maximum extractable amount at various fee rates?
Does this affect DeFi integrations that rely on standard ERC20 allowance semantics?
validation_requirements: Trace exact balance and allowance changes through transferFrom with non-zero fee
Calculate maximum extraction: at 100% fee, approval of X allows extracting 2X from sender
Test with standard DeFi patterns (approve + transferFrom)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Sender approves spender for 1000 EURF. Fee rate is 1% (100 bps). Spender calls transferFrom(sender, recipient, 1000). _payTxFee deducts 10 EURF fee from sender (no allowance check). super.transferFrom transfers 1000 EURF from sender and consumes 1000 from allowance. Sender loses 1010 EURF total but only approved 1000. At 100% fee rate, spender extracts 2000 EURF with a 1000 approval.
prerequisites: Non-zero fee rate must be set
Any user with any ERC20 approval can exploit

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Every transferFrom extracts extra tokens beyond approval. At maximum fee rate (100%), impact doubles the approved amount. Breaks all DeFi integrations relying on allowance semantics.
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users who have granted any ERC20 approvals
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires contract upgrade to fix; extracted tokens are in feesFaucet

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test for total deduction vs allowance comparison in fee-bearing transferFrom

validation_result.mechanism: Fee deduction bypasses ERC20 allowance in transferFrom
validation_result.impact_summary: In transferFrom, _payTxFee deducts fees from the sender via _update() without consuming allowance. The spender can force the sender to lose more tokens than approved (amount + fees vs. approved amount only).
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The transferFrom function in Token.sol (line 168-171) calls transferSanity before calling super.transferFrom. transferSanity (line 163-166) calls _payTxFee which uses _update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) to transfer fees directly from the sender's balance. This _update() call is a raw balance transfer — it does not check or consume any ERC20 allowance. Subsequently, super.transferFrom (OpenZeppelin's ERC20Upgradeable.transferFrom) calls _spendAllowance(sender, spender, amount) which only consumes 'amount' from the allowance, not 'amount + txFees'. Therefore, the total tokens removed from the sender (amount + txFees) exceeds the allowance that was approved and consumed (amount). This is a direct violation of the ERC20 allowance invariant: a spender should never be able to cause a sender to lose more tokens than the sender approved.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal at Token.sol lines 129-138 is accurate. Line 132 shows `_update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees)` which bypasses allowance. Lines 168-171 show `transferFrom` calling `transferSanity` then `super.transferFrom`, confirming the fee is deducted outside the allowance mechanism. OpenZeppelin's ERC20Upgradeable.transferFrom internally calls `_spendAllowance(from, spender, value)` which only reduces allowance by `value` (the transfer amount), not `value + fees`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: transferSanity deducts fees from sender without allowance check, then super.transferFrom only consumes 'amount' from allowance. Total deducted from sender = amount + fees, but allowance only covers amount.
_update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) directly moves tokens from sender to feesFaucet without any allowance check or consumption. This is called before super.transferFrom in the transferFrom path.
This internal function is called from transferFrom before super.transferFrom, deducting fees from sender's balance without considering allowance.

calling_sequence: 1. Spender calls transferFrom(sender, recipient, amount) at Token.sol:168
2. transferSanity(sender, recipient, amount) called at Token.sol:169
3. adminSanity(sender, recipient) checks pause/blacklist at Token.sol:164
6. Balance check: balanceOf(sender) >= txFees + amount at Token.sol:131
7. _update(sender, _feesFaucet, txFees) transfers fees WITHOUT allowance check at Token.sol:132
8. super.transferFrom(sender, recipient, amount) called at Token.sol:170
9. OZ ERC20Upgradeable._spendAllowance(sender, msg.sender, amount) — only consumes 'amount' from allowance
10. OZ ERC20Upgradeable._transfer(sender, recipient, amount) — transfers 'amount' from sender
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical defect is at step 7: _update(from, _feesFaucet, txFees) at Token.sol:132 moves txFees tokens from sender to feesFaucet using the internal _update function which is a raw balance modification. It does not call _spendAllowance. Then at step 9, super.transferFrom only calls _spendAllowance for 'amount', not 'amount + txFees'. The net effect: sender loses (amount + txFees) tokens, but only (amount) of allowance is consumed. The fee portion bypasses the allowance entirely.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (deployToken.js) do not set _txfeeRate or _feesFaucet during deployment. These are configured post-deployment by ADMIN. The vulnerability is active whenever both _txfeeRate > 0 and _feesFaucet != address(0), which are expected operational configurations for a fee-bearing token.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is fully valid. Example: (1) Sender approves spender for 1000 EURF. (2) ADMIN has set fee rate to 1% (100 bps) and feesFaucet to a valid address. (3) Spender calls transferFrom(sender, recipient, 1000). (4) _payTxFee computes txFees = 1000 * 100 / 10000 = 10 EURF. (5) _update(sender, feesFaucet, 10) deducts 10 EURF from sender — no allowance consumed. (6) super.transferFrom deducts 1000 EURF from sender and consumes 1000 from allowance. (7) Sender loses 1010 EURF total but only approved 1000. At 100% fee rate (10000 bps, which setTxFeeRate allows), approval of N tokens lets spender extract 2N tokens from sender.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Every transferFrom call with non-zero fee rate extracts more tokens from the sender than their approved allowance. At 1% fee rate, a 1000-token approval allows extracting 1010 tokens. At 100% fee rate (10000 bps, which is allowed by setTxFeeRate), a 1000-token approval allows extracting 2000 tokens. This breaks all DeFi integrations relying on standard ERC20 allowance semantics (DEXes, lending protocols, routers, etc.). Any user who has granted any ERC20 approval is affected. The defect exists in the core transferFrom function and cannot be mitigated without a contract upgrade.
recommended_mitigations: Include the fee in the allowance consumption: call _spendAllowance(sender, _msgSender(), amount + txFees) before the fee deduction, or modify super.transferFrom to account for fees
Alternative: deduct fees from the transferred amount rather than adding to it (i.e., recipient receives amount - fees, sender loses exactly amount)
Add a test that verifies total balance deduction from sender in transferFrom never exceeds the approved allowance
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy EURFToken via UUPS proxy. 2. Initialize and set ADMIN. 3. ADMIN calls setFeeFaucet(feeCollector) and setTxFeeRate(100) (1% fee). 4. Mint 1010 tokens to sender. 5. Sender approves spender for exactly 1000 tokens. 6. Spender calls transferFrom(sender, recipient, 1000). 7. Verify sender balance decreased by 1010 (1000 + 10 fee). 8. Verify allowance remaining is 0 (only 1000 consumed). 9. Confirm 1010 tokens left sender but only 1000 was approved — violation proven.
additional_testing_needed: Test with maximum fee rate (10000 bps = 100%) to confirm 2x extraction
Test repeated transferFrom calls to verify cumulative over-extraction
Test interaction with infinite approval (type(uint256).max) to see if the issue still applies (OZ skips _spendAllowance for infinite approval, but fee is still extra)
